feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: What is the difference between Actual costing and material cost estimate?
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Could anyone please help clarify the difference between Actual Costing (CKMLCP) and Material Cost Estimate (CK11N)?

From my understanding, both seem to update the material cost, which is where my confusion arises. I’m trying to understand:

  • What is the specific purpose of each?
  • When should each be used?
  • How do both processes coexist without conflict?

I would really appreciate a detailed explanation to help me better understand this.

Thank you in advance!

Best Regards,

Nithin

entry.title: Clarification on Downpayment Payment Workflow Behavior in SAP
entry.content:
I would like to understand how to cancel parked documents created in the *Verify Supplier Down Payment Requests, particularly when the verification has been rejected.

Unfortunately, the flow described here does not provide any information on handling parked documents:Approval Workflows for Supplier Down Payment Requests | SAP Help Portal

I also attempted to use the *Display Parked Document, but received the error message:'Document 2210/15000000XX/2025 was created using Fiori applications; no processing.' The message suggests using the *Verify General Journal Entry, but the document cannot be selected there.

Is there a way to cancel a parked document for a supplier down payment request?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: 2ET: Correct CBC Org Structure for Sales Org / Plant / Shipping Point?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am a junior consultant working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and I am currently documenting and testing several scope items for a sales scope we want to offer. My current focus is 2ET – Sales Order Processing for Non-Stock Material.

1. What exactly do we do in the system?

I tested the 2ET process in our customizing tenant and compared the findings with our development tenant.

Systems:

  • Stark Tower = customizing tenant

  • Arc Reactor = development tenant

Relevant process / step:

  • Scope item 2ET – Sales Order Processing for Non-Stock Material

  • especially the organizational prerequisites needed so sales processing works correctly

Relevant apps / objects:

  • Manage Sales Orders – Version 2

  • CBC Organizational Structure

  • CBC: Assign Sales Organization - Distribution Channel - Plant

  • shipping-point related CBC activities such as:

    • Define Shipping Point

    • Assign Shipping Points

    • Assign Shipping Points According to Storage Location

    • Define Rule for Determination of Shipping Point

My original operational issue was that in sales-order processing I could not get a usable shipping-point result for DE01, so I started checking and adjusting the setup in the customizing tenant. That led me deeper into CBC and the organizational structure.

2. What do I expect?

I expect that if the organizational structure is built correctly, then a sales organization such as DE01 should appear in CBC activity "Assign Sales Organization - Distribution Channel - Plant" and should also function correctly in the application afterwards.

My goal is to understand the minimum required org structure / hierarchy so I know when the sales organization should appear in that CBC configuration and when it becomes functionally usable in the system. This is the main reason for my question.

3. What actually happens?

What I see is inconsistent:

  • In Organizational Structure, DE01 is visible:

    • Company Code DE01

    • Sales Organization DE01

    • Sales Area DE01 / DE / 01

    • Plant DE01

    • Shipping Point DE01

    • Storage Location DE01

  • However, in CBC activity "Assign Sales Organization - Distribution Channel - Plant", DE01 is not listed at all.

A colleague explained to me that this assignment only appears if the hierarchy is correct and complete. If that is true, then DE01 may be visually present in Organizational Structure, but still structurally incomplete from CBC’s point of view.

That is exactly the inconsistency I am trying to understand.

4. Suspected cause

My current assumption is that the issue is caused by an inconsistent org structure / hierarchy in the customizing tenant.

More specifically:

  • parts of the structure were visible, but not all relationships seemed equally solid

  • DE01 did not appear in the CBC assignment where I would expect it

  • earlier we also saw cases where plant / shipping-related relationships looked disconnected or incomplete in Stark compared with Arc

At one point we also worked on shipping-point-related setup and org connections, because the issue first looked like a shipping-point problem. Later, a colleague suggested comparing the setup with the development tenant. In that comparison, the Arc Reactor structure looked much cleaner and more coherent than Stark Tower.

Development vs. customizing comparison

In Arc Reactor, the structure looks like a proper reference:

  • Sales Organization GE01 is connected cleanly

  • Sales Area GE01 / 10 / 00 is linked properly

  • Plant GE01 is integrated and linked with shipping points, storage locations, sales organizations, and purchasing organization

  • the corresponding CBC assignment is present

In Stark Tower, DE01 is visible in Organizational Structure, but the expected CBC-generated assignment is missing. That is why I suspect the issue is not just transactional, but structural.

My questions

  1. Which minimum org structure is required so that a sales organization appears in "Assign Sales Organization - Distribution Channel - Plant"?

  2. Which relationships must be correct between:

    • Company Code

    • Sales Organization

    • Sales Area

    • Distribution Channel

    • Division

    • Plant

    • Shipping Point

    • Storage Location

  3. If all those objects are visible in Organizational Structure, but the sales organization is still missing in that CBC activity, what does that usually mean?

  4. Does that clearly indicate an incomplete or inconsistent org hierarchy?

  5. Is there a reference example of a minimal correct hierarchy for such a setup in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

I attached screenshots from both systems and labeled them clearly as:

  • Arc Reactor (development tenant)

  • Stark Tower (customizing tenant)

My goal is not just to find a workaround, but to understand the correct structural prerequisite so I can rebuild or correct the customizing setup properly.

Thank you.

Screenshots:

Organizational Structure Customizing System (Stark Tower):

___________________________________________________________

Organizational Structure Development System (Arc Reactor):

___________________________________________________________

Assign Sales Organization - Distribution Channel – Plant (Configuration)

entry.title: Interest rate does not match interest postings
entry.content:
I tried to check several notes and blogs about interest calculation for SAP Lease Accounting but I am not able to match the values. Please confirm the formula SAP is using to derive the interest amounts for this contract.

Contract is from 01.01.2026 to 31.12.2026. Simple monthly payments of 1,000 with 3% interest rate at 30 days per month.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/fiaccounting/3361878949.html?locale=en-US

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3504599

https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-q-a/lease-interest-is-calculated-monthly-although-frequency-rule-is-set-to/qaq-p/13616249

entry.title: Restricting over goods receipt quantity during CO11N confirmation
entry.content:
Hi,

During production order confirmation in CO11N / Confirm Production Order Operation, the business uses automatic goods receipt.

The expectation is that the goods receipt quantity should not exceed the production order quantity.

Thanks and regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Restrictions by company code and Plant in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
The client needs to restrict access at the company code and Plant level based on the end user's line of business to ensure they only have visibility in their current location within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

The client has 11 Plants and 2 company codes.

Let's assume the end user belongs to the Purchasing business line and is assigned to the Mexico City plant, which represents 1010 in SAP, while the company code is 1000.
The following standard roles are those used by the end user to run Fiori applications in the purchasing area.

Roles estándar

BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST

BR_CASH_MANAGER

BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT

BR_EMPLOYEE

BR_INVENTORY_MANAGER

BR_MATL_PLNR_EXT_PROC

BR_RECEIVING_SPECIALIST

BR_AA_ACCOUNTANT

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT_MX

BR_BPC_EXPERT

BR_PURCHASER_RFM

After that, I entered an app called “Maintain Business Roles” (F1492)

Roles in blue generated a copy of the standard because they can be restricted at the plant level (WERKS) and the company code level (BUKRS).

Roles Copia 1

Restriccion

BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST

 

BR_CASH_MANAGER_CDMX_1010

2000

BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_EMPLOYEE

 

BR_INVENTORY_MANAGER_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_MATL_PLNR_EXT_PROC_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_RECEIVING_SPECIALIST_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_AA_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010

1010, 1000

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT_MX_CDMX_1010

2000

BR_BPC_EXPERT_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_PURCHASER_RFM_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

Taking as an example one of the 10 roles, BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT, which generates 2 copies, the standard role has no restrictions, but the 2 copies as BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010 and BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010_V2 do have restrictions.

I clicked on edit and for the BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010 role I started with the restrictions.

Then click on “Maintain Restrictions”

In the “Access Categories” section, select “Restricted” for Write, Read, Value Help, and in the Assigned Restriction Types section, click on “general” where you will find the Company code and Plant fields.

For the company field, it is necessary that the end user only see the information for company code 1000, so only place the check mark for 2000 as a restriction.

In the plant field, select all plants except 1010, which represents Mexico City, because I want the user to only have access to that plant and be restricted from the others: 1020, 1030, 1040, 1050, 1060, etc.

Finally, I clicked the "Save" button to save the changes.

I repeated the steps with the rest of the roles, creating a copy respectively, in which I only restricted the company code and the plant

Roles estándar

Roles Copia 1

BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST

BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST

BR_CASH_MANAGER

BR_CASH_MANAGER_CDMX_1010

BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT

BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010

BR_EMPLOYEE

BR_EMPLOYEE

BR_INVENTORY_MANAGER

BR_INVENTORY_MANAGER_CDMX_1010

BR_MATL_PLNR_EXT_PROC

BR_MATL_PLNR_EXT_PROC_CDMX_1010

BR_RECEIVING_SPECIALIST

BR_RECEIVING_SPECIALIST_CDMX_1010

BR_AA_ACCOUNTANT

BR_AA_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT_MX

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT_MX_CDMX_1010

BR_BPC_EXPERT

BR_BPC_EXPERT_CDMX_1010

BR_PURCHASER_RFM

BR_PURCHASER_RFM_CDMX_1010

However, when logging into the Manage Purchase orders app F0842A

I still have visibility with the other plants and I only want it to have access to the 1010 in CDMX, could you guide me on the correct restriction process? Please

entry.title: Exclude condition types from Profit Margin Calculation
entry.content:
In a pricing procedure, how can I exclude specific condition types from the profit margin calculation?

I have some surcharge condition types set up in my pricing procedure, which the customer needs to pay for, but these conditions must not affect the profit margin calculation for PCIP, is there a way to achieve this?

The 4 conditions selected are the ones that should be charged to the customer but not be part of the profit margin calculation.

entry.title: Notification alerts for an export dispatch
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

We have the reqeuirements from the client as follows: 

Rqeusting you to provide me the exact configuration guidance to map the scenario in SAP Public Cloud 2602.

1. When an order is received for export sales, a notification should be sent to the RM/PM Department.

2. Upon receiving the alert, the department will check the availability of RM/PM.

3. After the availability check, a notification—whether RM/PM is available or not—should be sent to the MD and other stakeholders.

4. If RM/PM is available, a notification should then be sent to the Production team to initiate manufacturing.

5. Once the product is ready, a notification should be sent to the MD, stakeholders, and the Dispatch team.

6. After billing is completed, a final notification should be sent to all stakeholders, including the MD.

7. The workflow should also include process tracking, highlighting any delays and specifying the number of delayed days.

 

Regards,

Tushar Sonawane

entry.title: Updated Key field length of database and when TR is released, error is occurring in RAP Eclipse
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm new in s4hana cloud public RAP.

I created RAP custom app in Eclipse and deployed to 080 dev tenant.

And later, I updated the key field length and added new field. Since it was able to update successfully, I though there is no problem.

But, now I need to transport/deploy that custom app to Test Tenant.

So, I release the Transport request/task in Eclipse and the following error is occurring.

Changes in ZC_ITEM(CDS_STOB) for Use in Key User Apps, compared to snapshot 100(0000), found by component BC-DWB-DIC: Key element KEY_NO was added.

Changes in ZC_ITEM(CDS_STOB) for Use in Cloud Development, compared to snapshot 100(0000), found by component BC-DWB-DIC: Key element KEY_NO was added. 

API Release: Compatibility of Released Objects

How can I fix that kind of issue quickly and what is the best practice solution?

 

Best Regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: Missing Journal in Billing SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Expert, 

I have a condition to make custom condition type to appear in pricing procedure and also the value will be appear in journal accounting document in the debit coloumn.

i already maintain it like this

ZTWU copy from TID1

This is the pricing procedure, ZTWU with spesific account key into specific G/L number

this is the Automatic account determination

and this is when i create the billing

 

Do you know where i'm missing about the configuration so the value of ZTWU will be appear in the Journal Debit Coloumn ?

 

Thankyou

entry.title: deactivation of company code
entry.content:
Hello experts,

 

My client wants to deactive the company code, what are steps to be followed before deactivation of existing company code.

 

what will be procedure in sap public cloud and links would be highly appriciated...?

entry.title: SAP TM Public Cloud: Is Round‑Trip Transportation with Empty Legs Supported?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am currently supporting a large SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation in which Transportation Management is a core component of daily inbound and outbound operations. I would appreciate peer guidance on a specific transportation planning scenario.

Business context

Our logistics model relies on land vehicles operating in continuous round-trip cycles:

  • An empty truck travels from Location A to Location B to collect material.
  • A full truckload then transports material from Location B to Location A, after which the cycle repeats. 

Question

From a business perspective, this should ideally be represented as a single round-trip execution. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition TM, I would therefore like to model a Freight Order with:

  • an empty leg (A → B) for collection
  • a loaded leg (B → A) for transportation 

However, this does not seem to be supported in Cloud Public.

In Private Cloud implementations, I have seen similar scenarios handled using Freight Order Building Rules to enable the planning of empty movements, together with Continuous Move optimisation to link loaded and empty stages into a single round‑trip execution.

We are operating strictly within SAP standard, using delivered Fiori applications (Transportation Cockpit, Manage Freight Orders…), and are deliberately avoiding custom developments.

Is this scenario currently outside the functional scope of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition TM, or is there a standard modelling approach recommended by SAP that I may be overlooking?

Any insights based on practical implementation experience would be highly appreciated.

Kind regards,

entry.title: Issue in Generating Intercompany Billing Request – Error in App Generate Intercompany Billi
entry.content:
Description:

Hello Team,

I am currently facing an issue while attempting to generate an intercompany billing request using the app "Generate Intercompany Billing Request" as part of the scope item 4AN – Intercompany Billing for Cross-Company Cost Accounting Postings.

Despite completing all required configuration steps as per the official setup instructions, the system returns an error during the creation of the intercompany billing request.

System Details:

  • System: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Scope Item: 4AN – Intercompany Billing
  • App Used: Generate Intercompany Billing Request
  • Business Scenario: Cross-company cost allocation requiring intercompany billing

Issue Description:

  • When executing the app Generate Intercompany Billing Request, the system fails to create the billing request.
  • An error message is triggered during processing (see attached screenshot/logs if applicable).
  • The process does not proceed to billing document creation.

Configuration Performed:

I have completed all necessary configuration steps as per the setup guide, including but not limited to:

  • Maintenance of intercompany billing settings
  • Assignment of company codes and business partners
  • Configuration of cost centers and cost allocations
  • Setup of relevant master data (customers, suppliers, materials if applicable)
  • Verification of cross-company code postings
  • Activation of required scope item (4AN)

Checks Already Performed:

  • Verified that all master data is correctly maintained and consistent across company codes
  • Ensured that all required organizational assignments are in place
  • Confirmed that the cost postings are successfully created between company codes
  • Reviewed configuration against the official documentation
  • Tested multiple scenarios with the same result

Expected Result:

The system should successfully generate an intercompany billing request based on the cross-company cost posting.

Actual Result:

The system returns an error and does not generate the intercompany billing request.

Request:

Could you please help identify:

  • The root cause of this issue
  • Any missing configuration or prerequisites
  • Whether there are known limitations or additional steps required

Attachments:

  • Screenshot of the error message
  • Configuration screenshots (if needed)
  • Sample document numbers (cost postings)

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,

entry.title: Where-Used List not working in Eclipse (ADT) for S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
I’m currently working in an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud environment and have encountered an issue with the Where-Used List functionality in Eclipse (ABAP Development Tools). Specifically, the feature does not seem to return complete results or is not functioning as expected when trying to analyze object dependencies.

For example, when I perform a Where-Used List on CDS view I_PURCHASEREQUISITIONTP, the system only returns a recently created class (ZCL_MF902_01_VN) which I confirmed is using this CDS view. However, it does not return older existing classes such as ZCJ_MF915_01, even though they are also consuming the same CDS view.

I understand that SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud has certain restrictions compared to on-premise systems, but I would like to confirm whether this behavior is by design, or if there are specific configurations, indexing steps, or authorizations required to make the Where-Used List return complete and accurate results.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue or found an alternative approach for dependency analysis in Public Cloud?

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud where approval mails are trigred from S/4HANA during the Sales Order approval
entry.content:
Issuer Details :We are using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud where approval emails are triggered from S/4HANA during the Sales Order approval process.

Requirement: When an approval email is sent, the email should contain a dynamic link that:Opens a specific Fiori applicationAutomatically passes the Sales Order number from the emailDisplays the application pre-filtered for that specific Sales Order

Problem Statement :

Currently, the approval email is sent with a static link to a Fiori application and a Sales Order number mentioned in the email body.However:The link does not pass the Sales Order number dynamicallyApprovers must manually search or filter the Sales Order in the applicationThis leads to usability issues and increases approval processing timeWe want to understand whether S/4HANA Public Cloud

entry.title: Fiscal variant change in Ledger 2L without data
entry.content:
For Company code 0062 Initial fiscal year variant was K4 on both ledgers (0L and 2L).

For reporting purposes, it has been requested that we change ledger 2L to V3 fiscal year variant.

Note: No postings have been made on this company code. But we get error message: The error message received suggests that there’s data.

entry.title: Inventory Packaging Materials Set-up guidance
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We have a customer who would like to inventory packaging items in their WH, they have EWM. We created a VERP product called     
PCKINVT, with 100 PC in Inventory. When we use the Pack Outbound Deliveries app and select that packaging material the inventory doesn't get reduced on the Post Goods Issue. We really need further instructions on how to configure the inventory to be consumed on Outbound Deliveries/PGI

entry.title: SAP S/4 Public How to assign a specific employee to an operation in a Maintenance order?
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I am currently working on a Maintenance Management implementation in S/4HANA Public Cloud and I’m looking for some guidance regarding Personnel Assignment at the Operation level and Capacity Leveling.

Current Scenario: We need to assign specific employees to Maintenance Order operations (e.g., assigning a specific task to "Sergio Bravo") and subsequently monitor their individual capacity/availability to execute the work.

What we have done so far:

  • We have configured and assigned employees using the app "Manage Teams and Responsibilities - For Work Center Asset Management" for a specific Work Center.

  • However, we are unable to see these assignments reflected in the standard maintenance apps or find a way to filter/view individual capacity based on these assignments.

Technical Questions:

  1. Is it possible to perform individual person-level assignment and capacity monitoring without Scope Item 43R (Resource Scheduling)?

  2. If Scope Item 43R is the only way to achieve this through the Maintenance Scheduling Board or Manage Work Center Capacity apps, are there any workarounds within the standard Core for tracking specific personnel availability?

  3. In the absence of 43R, what is the best practice for visibility of assigned technicians to specific operations in Public Cloud?

Any insights, documentation, or "Best Practices" regarding this specific requirement would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your help!

Best regards,

Sergio Bravo

entry.title: Requirement of Custom Workflow of Integration of SD & PP Module In SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We received one requirement which need to map on SAP S/4 HANA Public cloud. The requirement is complex and in case of MTO which the SD & PP integration.

Please help and suggest the solution.

Requirement 

Required custom work flow to map MTO process with approval of 3 level the requirement is custom as no specific standard workflow available in SAP Public cloud after analysis sharing this with you 

Below are the discussed way-forward

1. Export End-to-End Process Flow

Step 1: Sales Order Creation

  • SAP S/4HANA Sales Order App
  • Mandatory Fields:
    • Export Order Type
    • Order Date
    • Customer Delivery Date (Mandatory)
  • Enhancement:
    • Add validation prevent save without delivery date

Step 2: Auto Notification to Stakeholders

Once order is saved:

Trigger Workflow / Notification to: Configuration basis.

  • Export Head
  • Procurement Manager
  • Logistics Manager
  • Production Manager
  • Export Billing Team
  • MD / COO

2. Department-wise Action Flow

Procurement (RM/PM Availability)

  • SAP Inbox Task
  • Action:
    • YES → RM/PM Available
    • NO → Enter Expected Availability Date

Production Planning

  • Triggered after Procurement update

Production Manager updates:

  • Production Start Date
  • Production Completion Date
  • FG Deposit Date

Logistics & Billing

After production completion:

  • Logistics:
    • Shipment Planning Date
    • Dispatch Date
  • Billing:
    • Invoice Date
    • Export Documentation Status

3. Status Tracking Structure

Create Status Flow Table



Stage

Status

Order Created

Completed

RM/PM Availability

Pending / Confirmed

Production

Planned / In Progress / Completed

Logistics

Planned / Dispatched

Billing

Completed

Final Status

Closed

4. Delay Alert Mechanism

Requirement:

If any department delays → Alert to ALL stakeholders

  • SAP Alert Notification Service

Logic:

  • Define SLA for each stage:
    • Procurement → 1 day
    • Production Planning → 2 days
    • Logistics → 1 day

If delay:

  • Trigger:
    • Email Alert
    • Fiori Notification
    • Escalation to MD / COO

Reporting:

  • Dashboard:
    • Order-wise status
    • Delay tracking
    • Department-wise performance

7. Workflow Logic (Simplified)

Order Created
   ↓
Notify All Stakeholders
   ↓
Procurement Update
   ↓
Production Planning Update
   ↓
Production Completion
   ↓
Logistics Planning
   ↓
Billing
   ↓
Order Closed

8. Automation Enhancements (Recommended)

  • Auto reminders every 6 / 12 / 24 hrs
  • Escalation Matrix:
    • Level 1 → Department Head
    • Level 2 → COO
    • Level 3 → MD

Final Outcome Expectation:

We will get:

  • Complete visibility of Export Orders
  • Department accountability
  • Automatic escalation system
  • Real-time tracking dashboard

Looking forward to hearing solution from you.

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: Clarification on STO and OBD Creation
entry.content:
Hi Team,

A concern was raised regarding STO creation with multiple line items for the same material, where delivery dates differ but the vendor remains the same.

Currently, when we run or attempt to create the OBD, the system generates separate OBDs for each line item based on the delivery dates. The question is whether it is possible to create a single OBD for all lines even if the delivery dates are different.

Please confirm if this is feasible, or share any alternative approach we can consider.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.
